problem.statement: Awnak errands and SHEIN assisted purchases require one governed journey from client intake through quote, WLT approval, operator execution, captain assignment and client readback.
problem.currentWorkaround: The client could create a request but could not reliably list, approve, cancel or follow its lifecycle, while operator controls were primarily read-only.

actors.role: Owner of an Awnak errand or SHEIN assisted-purchase request
permittedActions: create owned special request with an idempotency key
approve a prepared quote through the governed WLT handoff
assign an eligible request to a captain

actors.role: Captain assigned to Awnak or SHEIN final-mile work
permittedActions: identify Awnak and SHEIN final-mile service type

surfaces.exclusionReason: The registry assigns JRN-022 to client, captain and control-panel; partner stores do not own Awnak or SHEIN assisted-purchase truth.
Field workforce does not create, quote, approve or dispatch JRN-022 requests.

outcome.statement: Each Awnak or SHEIN request has one versioned DSH lifecycle, one governed WLT payment handoff when quoted, one dispatch linkage and immediate readback on every affected surface.
outcome.baselineState: Creation existed, but client lifecycle controls and operator execution actions were incomplete or disconnected.
outcome.targetState: Client, operator and captain surfaces consume the same generated contract, backend state model and canonical readback.
outcome.observationWindow: independent product, runtime, accessibility and financial-boundary review

criteria: Client creation is idempotent and validates required SHEIN or Awnak data.
Client can list, refresh, approve an eligible quote and cancel an eligible owned request.
Operator can prepare a quote, apply an expected-version transition and assign an eligible captain.
SHEIN dispatch is rejected before ready_for_delivery with structured blocking reasons.
Captain inbox distinguishes Awnak and SHEIN final-mile assignments.
WLT remains the only financial truth owner and DSH stores references only.
Every mutation is followed by canonical DSH readback.

Binary media is not required by the current discovery model; product URL, structured item data and address references are the required request evidence. Any future attachment capability requires an approved media-owner extension.

business: DSH owns operational request state while WLT owns payment truth.
Every successful write is followed by canonical readback.
SHEIN cannot dispatch before purchase, inbound, sorting and readiness timestamps are complete.
Captain assignment retains the specialRequestId and requestType linkage.
negative: No client can read or mutate another client request.
No surface can calculate or persist final financial truth.
No local array, mock or fallback can represent runtime request state.
No operator can bypass the workflow-stage model.
No captain can receive an unready SHEIN request.

execution.appetite: Close JRN-022 code gaps without creating a second media subsystem, mutating WLT truth, deploying production or activating commercial SaaS.
fixedConstraints: Repository mode is REMOTE_ONLY on sambassam.
DSH shared special-requests owns frontend business coordination.
WLT remains the sole financial truth owner.
No PR, merge, force push, release or production deployment is authorized.
